title: Harriet Tubman and the Underground Railroad
titleZh: 哈丽特·塔布曼与地下铁路

q.en: Around what year was Harriet Tubman born?
q.zh: 哈丽特·塔布曼大约出生在哪一年？

options.en: Around 1900 according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 约1900年（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Around 1820

explain.en: The book says, 'Harriet was born as a slave around 1820.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'哈丽特约于1820年出生为奴隶。'

q.en: In the glossary, what was the 'Underground Railroad'?
q.zh: 词汇表中，Underground Railroad（地下铁路）是什么？

options.en: The system of houses and safe places that helped slaves escape north to freedom
options.zh: 由房屋和安全地点组成的系统，帮助奴隶逃往北方获得自由

options.en: A group of slave owners
A real train that ran underground according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一列真正在地下运行的火车（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: A war between the North and South

explain.en: The glossary defines Underground Railroad as 'the system of houses and other safe places that provided a way for slaves to escape north to freedom (p. 11).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 Underground Railroad 定义为'由房屋和其他安全地点组成的系统，为奴隶逃往北方提供路径（第11页）'。

q.en: How did Harriet Tubman make her first escape to freedom?
q.zh: 哈丽特·塔布曼第一次逃向自由是怎么做到的？

options.en: She rode a train with her husband
She walked about 100 miles alone, traveling at night and hiding during the day
options.zh: 她独自走了约100英里，夜间赶路、白天躲藏

options.en: She disguised herself as a white man according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 她把自己装扮成白人男子（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: She shipped herself in a wooden box
options.zh: 她把自己装进木箱运输

explain.en: The book says, 'To escape, Harriet walked 100 miles (160 km), alone, through unknown land. She traveled at night and hid during the day.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'为了逃亡，哈丽特独自走了100英里（160公里），穿越陌生的土地。她夜间赶路，白天躲藏。'

q.en: In the glossary, what is an 'abolitionist'?
q.zh: 词汇表中，abolitionist（废奴主义者）是什么？

options.en: A slave owner on a plantation
A conductor on the Underground Railroad
options.zh: 地下铁路上的 conductor（向导）

options.en: A soldier in the Union Army
A person who fought to make slavery illegal
options.zh: 为废除奴隶制而奋斗的人

explain.en: The glossary defines abolitionists as 'people who fought to make slavery illegal (p. 8).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 abolitionists 定义为'为废除奴隶制而奋斗的人（第8页）'。

q.en: After the 1850 Fugitive Slave Act, where did Harriet have to lead escaping slaves to find freedom?
q.zh: 1850年《逃亡奴隶法》之后，哈丽特必须把逃亡的奴隶带往哪里才能找到自由？

options.en: Back to their owners
All the way to Canada
To a plantation in the South
To the nearest Northern city only

explain.en: The book says, 'Now Harriet had to conduct her passengers all the way to Canada to find freedom.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'现在哈丽特必须把乘客一路带到加拿大才能找到自由。'

q.en: In the glossary, what does 'perilous' mean?
q.zh: 词汇表中，perilous（危险的）是什么意思？

options.en: Filled with danger
Making fun of by imitating
Very little
A large farm

explain.en: The glossary defines perilous as 'filled with danger (p. 13).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 perilous 定义为'充满危险（第13页）'。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To describe only the Fugitive Slave Act
To argue that the Civil War was unnecessary according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 主张内战没有必要（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To tell the story of Harriet Tubman's bravery and the Underground Railroad that freed enslaved people
options.zh: 讲述哈丽特·塔布曼的勇敢事迹，以及让奴隶获得自由的地下铁路

options.en: To teach how to farm on a plantation

explain.en: The book follows Harriet from slavery to freedom and beyond, calling her 'a true hero' who 'led about three hundred slaves north to freedom,' showing a biographical, informative purpose.
explain.zh: 本书从哈丽特的奴隶生活讲到她获得自由及之后，称她为'真正的英雄'，'带领约三百名奴隶逃往北方自由'，体现传记性、说明性目的。

q.en: On the Underground Railroad, what did the code word 'Freight' mean?
q.zh: 在地下铁路上，暗语'Freight'是什么意思？

options.en: The people helping slaves escape
The routes north
Escaping slaves
Safe houses

explain.en: The book's code-word list says, 'Freight meant escaping slaves.'
explain.zh: 书中暗语表写道：'Freight 指逃亡的奴隶。'

q.en: About how many enslaved people did Harriet Tubman lead north to freedom?
q.zh: 哈丽特·塔布曼大约带领多少名奴隶逃往北方获得自由？

options.en: About one thousand
About three hundred
About ten
None—she only helped herself
options.zh: 一个也没有——她只帮了自己

explain.en: The book states, 'Harriet led about three hundred slaves north to freedom.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'哈丽特带领约三百名奴隶逃往北方获得自由。'

q.en: What did Harriet Tubman do during the Civil War?
q.zh: 内战期间哈丽特·塔布曼做了什么？

options.en: She worked as a nurse for the Union Army and as a spy
options.zh: 她担任联邦军的护士，并当间谍

options.en: She fought as a general in the Confederate Army according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她作为将军在邦联军作战（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She stayed hidden in Canada
She wrote the Fugitive Slave Act

explain.en: The book says, 'During the war, Harriet worked as a nurse for the North's Union Army. She was also a spy, scouting out the Southern army's weapon warehouses.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'战争期间，哈丽特担任北方联邦军的护士。她还是一名间谍，侦察南方军队的武器仓库。'

prompt.en: Harriet Tubman was called the 'Moses of her people.' Draw a map showing the route of the Underground Railroad from the South to freedom in the North or Canada, and label at least three 'stations' or code words from the book.
prompt.zh: 哈丽特·塔布曼被称为'她人民的摩西'。画一张地下铁路从南方通往北方或加拿大自由之地的路线图，并标出书中至少三个'车站'或暗语。

en: Use the 'Do You Know?' code-word box: Freight, Stations, Conductors, Lines.
Show the journey ending in Canada after the 1850 Fugitive Slave Act.
zh: 画出1850年《逃亡奴隶法》后终点为加拿大的旅程。
